articles.title: Football quiz: Name the north London derby's top scorers
articles.summary: With the north London derby coming up on Sunday, can you name the top scorers across Arsenal and Tottenham's Premier League meetings?

articles.title: Watkins has to respond to pressure of Abraham's arrival at Villa - Rooney
articles.summary: Match of the Day pundit Wayne Rooney believes Tammy Abraham's arrival at Aston Villa could be key to Ollie Watkins rediscovering his form, as the pressure of competition for his place in the starting ...

articles.title: O'Reilly scores twice as Man City beat Newcastle
articles.summary: Manchester City move to within two points of Premier League leaders Arsenal with a tense victory against Newcastle United at Etihad Stadium.

articles.title: Ten-man Chelsea drop points again as Burnley score stoppage-time equaliser
articles.summary: Chelsea make it 17 points dropped from winning positions in the Premier League this season as Zian Flemming's 93rd-minute header gives Burnley a 1-1 draw, after Wesley Fofana is sent off at Stamford B...

articles.title: Late Abraham goal rescues point for Villa against Leeds
articles.summary: Tammy Abraham comes off the bench to score an 88th minute equaliser to cancel out Anton Stach's spectacular freekick, as Aston Villa rescue a 1-1 draw against Leeds United at Villa Park in the Premier...

articles.title: Brighton ease pressure on Hurzeler with win at Brentford
articles.summary: Goals from Diego Gomez and Danny Welbeck help Brighton to their first league win in six games, as Fabian Hurzeler's side see off Brentford 2-0 at the GTech Community Stadium.

articles.title: West Ham held to goalless draw by Bournemouth
articles.summary: Third-bottom West Ham spurn the chance to put pressure on the teams above them in the table as they are held to a 0-0 draw at home to Bournemouth.

articles.title: 'Educate yourself and your kids' - Fofana and Mejbri racially abused
articles.summary: Burnley midfielder Hannibal Mejbri and Chelsea defender Wesley Fofana have spoken out on social media after being sent racist abuse in the aftermath of the clubs' Premier League match on Saturday.

articles.title: Red cards and poor home form - Chelsea 'set fire to points'
articles.summary: Chelsea have more red cards than any side in the Premier League this season, and boss Liam Rosenior says they have "set fire" to points.

articles.title: 'Drive and hunger' take Milner to Premier League appearance record
articles.summary: Brighton's James Milner starts against Brentford to break the all-time Premier League appearance record in his 654th match.

articles.title: Deal with 'noise' or join another club - Arteta
articles.summary: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta says his players should "be part of a different club" if they can not deal with the "noise" as his team bid to end their 22-year wait for a league title.

articles.title: Narrow wins can be 'bedrock of success', Hearts boss says
articles.summary: Heart of Midlothian head coach Derek McInnes suggests history shows narrow wins can be the difference between success and failure as his Scottish Premiership leaders beat Falkirk.

articles.title: 'Like a dream' - Abraham relishing Villa return
articles.summary: Seven years since firing them into the Premier League, Tammy Abraham is back scoring goals at Aston Villa to keep their fans and players dreaming of glory.

articles.title: Tottenham & Newcastle eye Nunez - Sunday's gossip
articles.summary: Tottenham and Newcastle want to bring Darwin Nunez back to the Premier League, Joe Gomez might leave Liverpool this summer, Roma and Juventus join the race for Marcos Senesi, plus more.

articles.title: Chelsea's set-piece defending 'too easy' to play against - Richards
articles.summary: Match of the Day pundit Micah Richards criticises Chelsea's inability to show "leadership and organisation" in defence, highlighting how Burnley were able to easily attack the home side from set-piece...

articles.title: Wrexham on march and West Brom's 'precarious position' - Sam Parkin's EFL takeaways
articles.summary: BBC Final Score pundit Sam Parkin delivers his verdict on the big talking points across the Championship, League One and League Two.

articles.title: Relegated in February? Why Sheff Wed are on brink of history
articles.summary: Sheffield Wednesday are on the brink of becoming the first team in English Football League history to be relegated in February.

articles.title: 'Commitment, honesty & endeavour' fuelling Wrexham
articles.summary: Phil Parkinson feels Wrexham's "sheer commitment, honesty and endeavour" was pivotal to their dramatic 5-3 win over Ipswich Town in the Championship.

articles.title: Watch: Comedy miss as leaders St Johnstone held by Raith Rovers
articles.summary: Watch as Sam Stanton's shot is cleared off the line by St Johnstone team-mate Josh Fowler during their Scottish Championship game against Raith Rovers.

articles.title: England, Luton & Ted Lasso - Powell's 40 years in football
articles.summary: Luton Town assistant boss Chris Powell talks to the BBC about his 40 years in football.
